serving.description: 1 can = 500ml (single serve as consumed in Singapore)
serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is 1 can (500ml / 500g), which is the standard single-serve format sold at Singapore convenience stores.

nutrient_source.title: Monster Energy Original — USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry; cross-referenced with Monster Beverage Singapore product label (500ml can)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods for Monster Energy Original. Values cross-referenced against the Singapore 500ml can label. Per-100g values derived from label per-serving data (500ml can). Protein and fat are confirmed zero by product formulation — this is a sugar-and-water-based carbonated beverage with no protein or fat ingredients. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and water content are not reported on the product label or in the USDA branded entry; set to -1 to indicate not reported by source.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of sucrose and glucose-fructose beverages — University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate applied to Monster Energy Original based on sucrose/glucose sugar composition
gi_source.source_match_notes: Monster Energy Original contains sucrose and glucose as primary carbohydrate sources. GI ~70 is assigned based on the University of Sydney GI Database reference values for sucrose (GI ~65) and glucose (GI ~100), yielding a blended estimate of ~70 for this sugar-base formulation. GL per 500ml can = (70 × 54g available carbohydrate) ÷ 100 = 37.8. No direct GI measurement for Monster Energy Original exists in the University of Sydney database; this is a defensible analogous estimate per Project Veritas GI policy.

hypertension.key_factor: 160mg caffeine per 500ml can plus 370mg sodium per can
hypertension.guidance: A single 500ml can delivers 160mg caffeine (equivalent to 2× Red Bull), which causes significant transient blood pressure elevation and may blunt antihypertensive medication efficacy; combined with 370mg sodium per can (~18% of the 2,000mg daily limit), Monster Energy poses a high concern for patients with hypertension and should be avoided or strictly limited.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: 54g sugar per can (GI 70, GL 37.8) — glycaemic equivalent of a full meal
type2_diabetes.guidance: One 500ml can contains 54g of sugar (13.5 teaspoons) with a glycaemic load of 37.8 — among the highest GL values of any single-serve beverage — making it equivalent to a full bowl of high-GI white rice in glycaemic impact; patients with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid Monster Energy entirely, and the 160mg caffeine content may additionally cause transient insulin resistance and impair postprandial glucose regulation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: 160mg caffeine, high sugar load (54g/can), and 370mg sodium per can
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Although saturated fat is 0g, the combination of 160mg caffeine (which increases heart rate and blood pressure transiently), 54g sugar per can (contributing to hypertriglyceridaemia and insulin resistance with habitual use), and 370mg sodium per can makes Monster Energy a high-concern beverage for patients with established cardiovascular disease; regular consumption is associated with adverse cardiac events in susceptible individuals and should be avoided.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Monster Energy Drink Original Green (500ml can) is a carbonated, sugar-sweetened energy drink manufactured by Monster Beverage Corporation (USA) and widely sold in Singapore at 7-Eleven, FamilyMart, Cold Storage, and NTUC FairPrice. It is ready-to-drink, served cold from a can, and has a sweet, slightly citrus-tart flavour with strong carbonation. The drink is ultra-processed and contains carbonated water, sucrose, glucose, citric acid, taurine (1,000mg per can), caffeine (160mg per can), and B-vitamins (riboflavin, niacin, B6, B12). Per 500ml can: 225 kcal, 54g sugar (13.5 teaspoons), 160mg caffeine, and 370mg sodium. The glycaemic load per can is approximately 37.8 — one of the highest GL values of any single-serve beverage, equivalent to a full bowl of white rice. The 160mg caffeine per can is double the caffeine of a standard 250ml Red Bull, and when combined with pre-workout supplements (150–300mg caffeine), total daily caffeine can easily exceed the 400mg/day maximum recommended by MOH Singapore. MOH Singapore advises that children, pregnant women, and individuals with cardiovascular conditions should not consume Monster Energy or similar high-caffeine drinks. Clinically, this beverage is high concern for hypertension (caffeine-mediated BP elevation plus sodium load), Type 2 Diabetes (extreme sugar and GL load), and cardiovascular disease (caffeine, sugar, and sodium combined). Commonly consumed in gym pre-workout, nightlife, gaming, and exam study contexts in Singapore.
common_user_queries: how much sugar in Monster Energy
is Monster Energy safe for diabetics

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods for Monster Energy Original and cross-referenced with the Singapore 500ml product label. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database reference values for sucrose and glucose, which are the primary carbohydrate sources in this product.
notes: Carbonated energy drink with caffeine, taurine, and B-vitamins, available in various flavours. Protein_g is confirmed zero — this is a carbohydrate-and-water-based beverage containing no protein ingredients by formulation. Fat_g is confirmed zero — no fat-containing ingredients are present in the formulation. Monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1 (not reported by source — neither the USDA branded entry nor the Singapore product label reports these sub-fractions for a zero-fat beverage). Water_g is set to -1 (not reported by source — the USDA branded entry does not report water content for this product). The 500ml single-serve format is the standard retail unit in Singapore; most consumers drink the entire can, receiving the full 160mg caffeine dose.
